text: Creatine monohydrate supplementation does not improve endurance performance in trained athletes, showing a trivial negative effect size.
Creatine monohydrate has robust evidence for enhancing short-term high-intensity exercise but not aerobic performance.
The lack of endurance benefit from creatine monohydrate is consistent across studies, with low heterogeneity in the meta-analysis.
Endurance athletes should not expect performance gains from creatine monohydrate supplementation for aerobic activities.
